meaning: adv.单独地,个别地,逐一地,直截了当地
examples: Guests arrived singly or in groups.
They marched out singly or in pairs.
The stamps are available singly or in books of ten.
Rather than fight battles singly, he prefers all-out war on what he sees as unreason.
Again, this mimics a singly-capable Tricorder device where hand-held electronics receive wireless signals about the temperature inside your body.
